calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-14-2025 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue, Evidentiary, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff George Varkis; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Stephan Choo; And Exhibits In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Jiani Jiang, Esq. 650-780-1678 Ropers Majeski, Esq. for Defendant BROOKFIELD PROPERTIES MULTIFAMILY, LLC.
No other Appearance.

Prior to Open court, Counsel states she is not contesting the tentative ruling and only seeks clarification on the deadline of responses.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentativ ruling. In addition respones are due 10 days from notice of entry of order.

DEFENDANT BROOKFIELD PROPERTIES MULTIFAMILY, LLC.'s Motion For Issue, Evidentiary, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff George Varkis.

Defendant Brookfield Properties Multifamily, LLC's Motion For Issue, Evidentiary, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff George Varkis is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Good cause appearing and the record demonstrating Plaintiff George Varkis misused discovery by failing to timely comply with the court's discovery orders, Plaintiff George Varkis is to pay to Defendant Brookfield Properties Multifamily, LLC the sum of $2,850 . payable by June 6, 2025, and Plaintiff George Varkis shall pay further sanctions of $1,500 to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by June 17, 2025, receipt filed no later than June 23, 2025.

Plaintiff and counsel are admonished to review the California Discovery Act and, going forward, comply with it, including their duties to timely respond to requests and reasonably and in good faith to try to resolve disputes informally.

At this time, the additional relief sought by Defendant is denied.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Defendant shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order setting out the above text verbatim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Set For Trial On Dec-01-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2025 Continued To Mar-05-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar Jan-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT BROOKFIELD PROPERTIES MULTIFAMILY, LLC's MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER INTERROGATORIES.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion was timely filed and unopposed. Within 15 days of service of order, Plaintiff is to provide further responses to interrogatories. Sanctions are also imposed jointly on Plaintiff's counsel and Plaintiff in the amount of $672.50. said sanctions also to be paid within 15 of service of order. The Court reduced the amount of sanctions requested as the original amount requested included time spent reviewing any opposition and appearing in court.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Barbara A. Zuniga; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/BAZ)

Motion To Compel Plaintiff George Varkis To Further Respond To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jiani Jiang; And Exhibits In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jan-07-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT BROOKFIELD PROPERTIES MULTIFAMILY, LLC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff George Varkis To Further Respond To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion was timely filed and unopposed. Within 15 days of service of order, Plaintiff is to provide further responses to special form interrogatories. Sanctions are also imposed jointly on Plaintiff's counsel and Plaintiff in the amount of $672.50. said sanctions also to be paid within 15 of service of order. The Court reduced the amount of sanctions requested as the original amount requested included time spent reviewing any opposition and appearing in court.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Barbara A. Zuniga; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/BAZ)
